name: Retail & Wholesale
description: Trade accounts and repeat shoppers for a bricks-and-mortar store — a Product Lines object, store and account-type fields, and a pipeline that ends at the opening order.
ai_context: You are a CRM assistant for a bricks-and-mortar retailer that also sells wholesale to trade accounts. Contacts are individual shoppers, loyalty members, and the buyers who place orders on behalf of a stockist; companies are the stockists, distributors and hospitality accounts themselves. A deal is one trade or wholesale order being negotiated, from first enquiry through the line sheet and pro forma to the opening order being placed. Never invent stock levels, wholesale prices, RRPs or minimum order quantities — quote only what is recorded on the product line or the deal.

automation_rules.name: Triage every new customer record
automation_rules.description: Decides early whether a new name is a retail shopper or a trade buyer, because the two get completely different follow-up.

params.title: Qualify {{contact.first_name}} {{contact.last_name}} — retail or trade? If trade, send the current line sheet and the account application
New customer added to {{org.name}}
params.body: Set Customer Type, Home Store and Marketing Opt-in before this record is used in any campaign. Trade buyers also need an Account Type and Payment Terms on the company record.

kb_templates.company:
## About us

We run [number] store(s) in [town/city] and supply [number] trade accounts. Replace this with what you sell, who you sell it to, and how long you have been trading.

## Stores

List each location with its address, opening hours and the manager's name — staff use this to answer "do you have it in the other shop?".

## Trade

Minimum opening order is [amount]. Standard trade discount is [%] off RRP, rising to [%] above [amount].
kb_templates.products:
## What we stock

- [Category] — our strongest line, [brief note on who buys it]
- [Category]
- [Category]

## Ranges

We buy two main seasons (Spring/Summer, Autumn/Winter) plus a core range that never drops out. Christmas is bought in [month].

## Own brand vs. brands we carry

Note which lines are exclusive to us — this is usually the reason an independent stockist chooses us over a distributor.
kb_templates.playbook:
## Qualifying a trade enquiry

1. Are they a shop, an online reseller or a distributor? Online resellers need a marketplace policy conversation first.
2. Where are they based, and do we already have a stockist within [distance]?
3. What do they currently stock in this category, and at what price point?
4. Can they meet the opening order minimum?

## Retail regulars

Anyone above [amount] a year gets tagged Gold and a call when their category lands. Take the marketing opt-in properly — no opt-in, no email, no exceptions.
kb_templates.process:
## From enquiry to opening order

1. **New Enquiry** — name captured in store, by phone or on the trade form. Log who they are and what they were asking about.
2. **Trade Application** — account form and VAT/company number received; run the credit check and set payment terms.
3. **Line Sheet Sent** — current season line sheet and price list with them, with the opening order minimum stated in writing.
4. **Showroom or Samples** — they have visited, or samples are out with them. Diarise the chase for [number] days later.
5. **Pro Forma Sent** — a priced order in front of them. This is the stage that stalls, so chase it twice.
6. **Opening Order Placed** — order confirmed and stock allocated. Hand over to the warehouse and set the re-order reminder.
kb_templates.competitors:
## Who we lose to

- **Distributors** — wider catalogue, one invoice. We win on exclusivity and lower minimums.
- **Direct-from-brand** — cheaper per unit at volume. We win on mixed pallets and no forward commitment.
- **The store down the road** — for retail customers, convenience beats everything. We win on stock depth, staff who know the product, and returns handled without a fight.

Replace these with the names you actually hear, and the one line that answers each.
